stdout:
**Completeness Against Criteria**

Blocking failure: the plan still does not make the `[hard]` “12 tasks, dependency waves” criterion executable as written. T5 says the scaffolding command applies the plan-quality preset to `P` (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:135-138`), but T6 is the task that defines that `plan-review` preset (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:139-142`). Both are in Wave 1, and T5 only depends on T1. An implementer assigned T5 cannot complete its stated behavior before T6 exists. This is a real dependency error in the fan-out plan.

The plan does address the prior B-ownership contradiction: scaffold now creates only `C` and `P` (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:49-55`, `135-138`), while T10 creates `B` (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:151-158`).

**Technical Soundness And Architectural Fit**

Blocking failure: T3 still misstates one of the graph primitives. The design says transitive traversal applies to `label-coverage` and `criteria-label-match` (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:80-82`, `120-123`). That is sound for `label-coverage`, whose current candidate set is direct adjacency through `children_of` (`crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:594-612`, `682-695`). It is not sound for `criteria-label-match`: that rule has no child traversal at all; it extracts criteria from the same issue and compares that same issue’s labels (`crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1179-1191`, `1227-1244`). Adding “transitive traversal” to it is not a completion of the bracket design; it would change or confuse a rule whose purpose is same-issue stray-label detection.

Blocking failure: the `validate --scope` boundary is under-specified in a way that can prevent the preview rule from firing. D13 says the preview `label-coverage` instance is keyed on `type:breakdown` and resolves `C` through `brackets:` (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:108`). T2 says `validate --scope <id>` evaluates a container bracket subtree “bounded at excluded-type nodes” (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:117-119`), while the coverage traversal design says `child-type-exclude` drops `type:breakdown` nodes and halts traversal through them (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:86-89`). If scoped validation excludes or stops before `B`, then a rule whose `when` selector matches `B` cannot run. The plan needs to specify whether scope includes `B` as an evaluation source while excluding it only from coverage candidates. Existing transition enforcement evaluates a concrete issue neighborhood and selected rules against materialized issues (`crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:614-639`, `722-748`); the proposed scoped path needs the same precision.

Advisory: T10 correctly recognizes that the current breakdown helper is parent-centric and cannot be reused as-is. The existing code copies the parent’s dependencies to every child, makes the parent depend on all children, and removes original dependencies (`crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:37-95`), which does not produce source/sink spine wiring.

**Decomposition And Dependencies**

Blocking failure: Wave 1 ordering is invalid. T5 needs T6 because it applies the plan-quality preset; T5 likely also needs T4 if `--with-planning` or `jit plan` is expected to resolve/configure the plan doc location. The current graph allows T5 to run after only T1 (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:131-142`), leaving its required gate/doc behavior unimplemented or re-derived.

Advisory: T7 and T8 examples depend on T3/T6, but adopting examples also need the `[planning]` config behavior from T1 and probably the scaffold behavior from T5 to be meaningful end-to-end. This is not as severe as the T5/T6 edge, but the wave description should make example prerequisites explicit.

**Risks And Actionability**

Blocking failure: the plan identifies the right lifecycle split for preview vs closure (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:108`, `172-175`), but the scoped validation mechanics are not actionable enough. An engineer still has to decide how `jit validate --scope <C>` selects the issue slice, which issue’s `when` selector is evaluated, how `B` is included, and how `child-type-exclude` interacts with scope traversal versus candidate traversal. Because the hard success criterion requires the breakdown gate to block uncovered `[hard]` criteria at fan-out time, this is load-bearing.

R5 is now correctly made mandatory rather than advisory (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:198-207`), and it matches the repo state: `.jit/config.toml` includes `planning`/`breakdown` in `[type_hierarchy]`, while `.jit/rules.toml` delegates write-path type validation to the frozen schema.

**Prior Review Feedback**

The prior blocking B-ownership contradiction is resolved. The prior `criteria-to-check` mistake is resolved: the plan now correctly keeps `criteria-to-check` per-issue (`dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:120-128`) matching the current implementation (`crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1015-1091` in the plan’s cited range). The prior preview/closure lifecycle gap is partially addressed with D13, but the new `validate --scope`/`B` inclusion ambiguity leaves the same gate-blocking requirement under-specified.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
